data.content:
# Handoff: implementation → implementation_review (foolery-aecb)

## Branch / artifact

- Branch: foolery-aecb-stale-grooming-timeout
- Commit: 1a3108c49f063d940ef9bcce4cab4d706b663c01
- Pushed to: origin/foolery-aecb-stale-grooming-timeout

## What landed

Lifted spawn / wire / timer plumbing out of
`src/lib/scope-refinement-prompt.ts` into a generic
`src/lib/agent-prompt-runner.ts` keyed on `subsystem` /
`subsystemLabel` / `timeoutMs` / `noOutputWarnMs`. Both subsystems
now call the runner with their own labels:

- scope-refinement keeps `[scope-refinement]` log tags, the existing
  `PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000`, and the
  "scope refinement agent timed out after 600s" error text.
- stale-grooming gets a separate
  `STALE_GROOMING_PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000` (independent constant),
  `[stale-grooming]` log tags, and
  "stale grooming agent timed out after Xs" /
  "stale grooming agent exited with code Y" error text.

`src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-job-runner.ts` now imports
`runStaleBeatGroomingPrompt` from
`@/lib/stale-beat-grooming-prompt` instead of
`runScopeRefinementPrompt`. Recorded grooming review failures
(`recordStaleBeatGroomingFailed` → `GET /api/beats/stale-grooming/status`)
therefore name stale grooming, never scope refinement.

## Acceptance-criterion mapping

1. AC1 (timeout error names stale grooming) — covered by
   `src/lib/__tests__/stale-beat-grooming-job-runner-timeout.test.ts`,
   "kills hung agent with stale-grooming-specific error message".
2. AC2 (independent timeout) — STALE_GROOMING_PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS
   exported from `src/lib/stale-beat-grooming-prompt.ts`, separate from
   `PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS` in `src/lib/scope-refinement-prompt.ts`. The
   shared runner accepts both as args; subsystem labels distinguish
   their error messages.
3. AC3 (distinguishable logs) — runner's spawn / first_byte / close /
   timeout / no_output / error log lines all start with
   `[<subsystem>][spawn]`. Asserted by
   "tags timeout log lines with [stale-grooming] subsystem" in the
   new test, and by `[scope-refinement]` assertions in the existing
   "processScopeRefinementJob: timeout" test.
4. AC4 (status endpoint records stale-grooming-specific failure) —
   recordStaleBeatGroomingFailed is invoked with the timeout message
   from runStaleBeatGroomingPrompt; the new test asserts the
   recorded error matches /stale grooming agent timed out/ and not
   /scope refinement/i.
5. AC5 (regression coverage + scope refinement parity) —
   `src/lib/__tests__/scope-refinement-worker.test.ts:processScopeRefinementJob: timeout`
   now also asserts the console.warn stream contains
   "scope refinement agent timed out after 600s" and
   "[scope-refinement]", and never "stale grooming". Both timeout
   suites pass under `bun run test`.

## Verification

- bun run lint — clean.
- bunx tsc --noEmit — clean.
- bun run test — 264 files, 2736 passed, 0 failed.
- bun run build — succeeded.
- Targeted re-runs:
  - bun run test -- src/lib/__tests__/stale-beat-grooming — 8 files,
    26 passed.
  - bun run test -- src/lib/__tests__/scope-refinement — 4 files,
    24 passed.

## Risks reviewer should sanity-check

- Behaviour parity for scope refinement: the existing 600s timeout
  test is the canary. Confirmed kept and now also asserts the
  scope-refinement label.
- Timer cleanup paths in the lifted safeResolve / safeReject —
  visually inspected; both clear timer and noOutputTimer before
  resolving / rejecting. No-output warn timer is no-op'd via the
  firstByteAt.value guard, matching prior behaviour.
- File / function / line caps satisfied (lint passed).

## Out of scope

- Knots workflow graph and terminal-state behaviour — untouched.
- Stale-grooming review state machine, queue, and worker loop —
  untouched.
- Subprocess spawn flags, env, or agent adapter wiring — untouched.
